# Resource upgrade

This topic describes the steps to complete the Kubernetes resource types upgrade, when a new version of Consul requires it.

## Overview

Kubernetes resources for Consul provide a way to configure and manage Consul on Kubernetes clusters. 
Consul 2.0.0 provides new resource types that offer enhanced functionality and improved compatibility with OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) versions 4.19 and above. 
These new resource types are part of the `consul.hashicorp.com` API group.

## Requirements

- OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) 4.19 or later
- Consul 2.0.0 or later

## Upgrade scenarios

To upgrade the resource types used by Consul in your Kubernetes cluster, update the API gateway resources from the current API resource group `gateway.networking.k8s.io` to the new `consul.hashicorp.com`.

This change is necessary if your application uses `TCPRoute` along with API gateway functionality. This is true on specific Kubernetes environments, like OpenShift or Gateway CRDs managed by the platform. Kubernetes clusters that do not use the `TCPRoute` resource can, but do not need to, upgrade their API gateway resources to the new API group. 

The resource upgrade cases are the following:

| Scenario | Compatible with `consul.hashicorp.com` | Recommended resource |
| -------- | -------------------------------------- | -------------------- |
| OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) Kubernetes clusters that use TCPRoutes and want to upgrade their cluster from 4.18 to 4.19+. | YES | `consul.hashicorp.com`      |
| Kubernetes clusters that use TCPRoutes and where Gateway CRDs are managed by the platform.                                      | YES | `consul.hashicorp.com`      |
| Kubernetes clusters that use TCPRoutes and where Gateway CRDs are not managed by the platform.                                  | YES | `gateway.networking.k8s.io` |

- OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) Kubernetes clusters that use TCPRoutes and want to upgrade their cluster from 4.18 to 4.19+.

This leaves three cases:

1. OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) Kubernetes clusters that use TCPRoutes and want to upgrade their cluster from 4.18 to 4.19+.

    Moving to the new `consul.hashicorp.com` custom API resource group is required to ensure compatibility with OpenShift 4.19+.

1. Any Kubernetes cluster that needs TCPRoutes for your application and Gateway CRDs are managed by the platform.

    Moving to the new `consul.hashicorp.com` custom API resource group is required.
    
1. Any Kubernetes cluster that uses TCPRoutes for your application and Gateway CRDs are not managed by the platform.
    
    Moving to the new `consul.hashicorp.com` is optional. Staying on the `gateway.networking.k8s.io` is recommended.

## Upgrade steps

To upgrade your Kubernetes resources for Consul, follow these steps:

### A. OpenShift

1. Update your Consul Helm chart values. 

    This Helm upgrade generates manifests and applies the stable `v1` API version by replacing the `v1alpha` API version of the `gateway.networking.k8s.io` resources and then applies the manifests. 
    
    Add this configuration to your `values.yaml` file

    ```yaml
    global:
        openshift:
            enabled: true
            upgradeTo419From418: true
            crds:
                enableTcpRoute: false
                consulapi:
                    enabled: true
    ```
    If `openshift version` is less than or equal to 4.18
    If `global.openshift.crds.enableTcpRoute` is true, then install the TCPRoute CRD under the `gateway.networking.k8s.io`.
    If `global.openshift.crds.consulapi.enabled` is true, then install all the CRDs under the API group `consul.hashicorp.com`.

    If `openshift version` is greater than or equal to 4.19
    If `global.openshift.crds.enableTcpRoute` is true, or `global.openshift.crds.consulapi.enabled` is true, then install all the CRDs under the API group `consul.hashicorp.com`.

1. Make backups of all the manifests after the Consul upgrade before proceeding to the OCP upgrade.

1. Delete the old CRDs and resources (recommendation from OCP upgrade flow). 

   Run the following command to delete the old CRDs:

    ```shell-session
    $ for i in gateways gatewayclasses httproutes grpcroutes tcproutes referencegrants; \
        do oc delete crd $i.gateway.networking.k8s.io; \
    done
    ```

4. Upgrade your OpenShift cluster to version 4.19 or later.

### B. Other Kubernetes clusters

1. Update your Consul Helm chart values:
    ```yaml
    global:
        crds:
            enableTcpRoute: false/true
            consulapi:
                enabled: true/false
    ```

    If `global.crds.enableTcpRoute` , install the TCPRoute under the `gateway.networking.k8s.io` and watches the GVK.
    If `global.crds.consulapi.enabled`, installs the Gateway CRDs under the `consul.hashicorp.com`.
